mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and searching of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes for deployment and the complexity of managing a distributed system.

steps.title: Add the Milvus Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Milvus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Milvus Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Milvus CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Milvus to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

```

> This command installs the Milvus CRDs.

steps.title: Install Milvus using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Milvus database using Helm with the specified version and namespace.

```bash

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

> This command installs Milvus in the 'milvus' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Milvus cluster by applying a sample Custom Resource (CR).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: 2.5.0
EOF

```

> This command creates a Milvus cluster with the specified configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: 2.5.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Milvus cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get milvuscluster -n milvus

```

> This command lists the Milvus clusters in the 'milvus' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> This command uninstalls the Milvus release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your data, upgrading the Milvus installation, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get milvuscluster -n milvus -o yaml > milvus-backup.yaml

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.6.0

kubectl get milvuscluster -n milvus

```

> Backup the current Milvus cluster configuration.
> Upgrade Milvus to version 2.6.0.
> Verify the Milvus cluster status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get milvuscluster -n milvus -o yaml > milvus-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Milvus service is not running or the network is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods using 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Milvus cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Milvus cluster is still initializing or has encountered an error during startup.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Milvus pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the Milvus pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM per node, and consider scaling your nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions for Milvus have not been installed.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command to install the CRDs: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for large-scale vector searches, added support for new indexing algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old indexing methods in favor of new optimized algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.4, ensure to update your indexing configurations to use the new methods.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus cluster listed with 'kubectl get milvuscluster -n milvus' showing the status as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: 2.5.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
